titlestmt.titleproper: New York State College of Agriculture Dept. of Vegetable Crops records21-35-1582
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-01-13 01:47:05 -0500.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: New York State College of Agriculture Dept. of Vegetable Crops records

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: New York State College of Agriculture. Department of Vegetable Crops

did.physdesc: Survey, research data, publications, maps, and subject files
did.abstract: Survey and research data, subject files, and other records deriving from the Department, and pertaining to vegetable science and industry in New York State.

Also, four Vegetable Crops/Fruit and Vegetable Science/Horticulture department seminar books, 1961-1972, 1972-1984, 1984-1995; 1996-2003, with listing of weekly seminars, presentations by graduate students, faculty and visitors, and signatures of attendees.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: In 1992, a Department of Fruit and Vegetable Science was established, combining the Departments of Vegetable Science and Pomology. On July 1, 2000, the Department of Horticulture was established, combining the Department of Fruit and Vegetable Science with the Department of Floriculture and Ornamental Horticulture.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Survey and research data, subject files, and other records deriving from the Department, and pertaining to vegetable science and industry in New York State.

Also, four Vegetable Crops/Fruit and Vegetable Science/Horticulture department seminar books, 1961-1972, 1972-1984, 1984-1995; 1996-2003, with listing of weekly seminars, presentations by graduate students, faculty and visitors, and signatures of attendees.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: New York State College of Agriculture Dept. of Vegetable Crops Records, #21-35-1582. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebooks, data and soil survey maps of Columbia county

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook and data sheets with Monroe County material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Data by farm, records 260-313 and maps

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Report of land utilization survey, second copy, 1st carbon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Canning crops records. Arranged by canning company and grower.

did.unittitle: Vegetable schools for growers and extension agents
Vegetable schools for growers and extension agents
Soils and vegetable crops management, papers by W.C. Barnes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains 1905 letter to Professor John Craig from Dr. Jabez Fisher of Fitchburg, Massachusetts. Generally credited as the pioneer of cucumber forcing in New England; describes late 1800's experiments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence with primarily Genesee County cabbage growers to assess problems

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Results, correspondence and includes some greenhouse experiments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence with the New York Seed Improvement Co-operative Association, Inc.

did.unittitle: Farmers' Institute for State Institution Farm Workers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sponsored by NYS Department of Agriculture and Markets. Contains program and correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Grow Better Cabbage" campaign of 1935, newsletters, articles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence with the Iron Age division of A.B. Farquhar Co., Ltd., York, Pennsylvania

did.unittitle: Publications from the National Association of Manufacturers, New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bulletin of the Committee on Agricultural Cooperation no.9-12, (1936), Special Bulletin December 1938 and July 1940, Report December 1938, Special Study no.2-4 (1937) and December 1938, Special Supplementary Study 1937-1938, Special Report 1938-1939

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence with Cornell University Placement Bureau to find summer workers through this program

did.unittitle: New York State Agricultural Goals Conference

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Held at Onondaga Hotel, Syracuse, New York. Programs and notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Department seminars in the Department of Vegetable Crops

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Department seminars in the Department of Vegetable Crops

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Department seminars in the Department of Vegetable Crops

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Department seminars in the Department of Horticulture

did.unittitle: Slide set #64a, Ray Sheldrake, Potato Tuber Defects
Schulers harvester, potatoes. La Hart and Normans stone picker
Plant growing, student gardens, cold frames, hot beds, etc.
Garden tools and cultivation, mulches and cover crops
Insects and diseases, general muck, machinery, etc.
Student gardens, weed and chemical controls
Harvesting, grading, marketing minor element deficencies
Potato test plots, irrigation: potato and other
Evolution of Plant Breeding at Cornell
Herbicide trial for root rot control
Dry bean variety trial, Agronomy Farm, Aurora
Black bean observation trial, Agronomy Farm, Aurora
Black dry bean variety or line trial, Aurora Farm, Aurora
Small white and pea bean variety trial, Agronomy Farm, Aurora
Annual report of subject matter of specialists
Annual reports and documents on Smith Lever extension
